pr.title: PR #113 — Failure Classification Framework v1
pr.description:
## Summary

Adds the **Failure Classification Framework v1**, the next link in the chain `… → Execution Attempt → Execution Outcome → Failure Classification`. Where an `ExecutionOutcome` records *what resulted* from an attempt, this records *why* a failed outcome failed, organizing failures into reusable `failure_type` / `category` / `severity`, with deterministic ordering, severity counts, category summaries, and immutable evidence bundles. Pure governance/representation — no repair engine, schedulers, workers, autonomous execution, approvals, workflow routing, or merge behavior.

Follows the established framework pattern (dataclasses + `Engine(artifacts_root)` + 4-file evidence bundle + create/show/export CLI + command-registry registration + test-selection mapping), so it reads like `execution_outcome.py`.

### Naming note (avoids a collision)
The repo already has an **unrelated** `src/axiom_core/runner/failure_classification.py` (PR #29 "Retry & Failure Classification Engine") and its `tests/test_failure_classification.py`. To avoid two identically-named modules with overlapping class names, this new framework lives at distinct paths:
- module: `src/axiom_core/failure_classification_framework.py`
- tests: `tests/test_failure_classification_framework.py`

The task-specified class names (`FailureClassification`, `FailureType`, …) and CLI names (`failure-classification-*`) are kept exactly. The pre-existing PR #29 module and its tests are **untouched**.

### New module: `src/axiom_core/failure_classification_framework.py`

```python
class FailureType(str, Enum):     # validation/test/review/execution/configuration/unknown _failure
class FailureCategory(str, Enum): # input, output, logic, environment, data, unknown
class FailureSeverity(str, Enum): # info, warning, error, critical

@dataclass
class FailureClassification:        # classification_id, outcome_id, failure_type, category, severity, summary, created_at
@dataclass
class FailureClassificationReport:  # report_id, classification_count, info/warning/error/critical_count, created_at, classifications
@dataclass
class FailureClassificationEvidence: # evidence_id, report_id, summary, created_at

class FailureClassificationEngine:
    create(classifications) -> dict   # validates, sorts, counts, persists, writes evidence
    get_report(report_id); list_reports(); export_report(report_id) -> md
```

- **Consumes `ExecutionOutcome`** via the required `outcome_id` on every classification (empty `outcome_id` → `ValueError`).
- **Severity counts** drive the report (`info`/`warning`/`error`/`critical`); the markdown export also emits a **category-count** section.
- **Deterministic ordering**: sort key `(created_at, outcome_id, classification_id)` — order-independent of input.
- **Explicit pass/fail**: report `passed` iff `error_count == 0 and critical_count == 0`.
- **Path safety**: `report_id` segment-validated (rejects empty/whitespace/`..`/`/`/`\`) and resolved inside an artifacts sandbox (rejects symlink escape).

### Evidence output
`artifacts/failure_classifications/<report_id>/`: `failure_classification_request.json`, `failure_classification_result.json`, `failure_classification_summary.md`, `pass_fail.json` (plus `report.json` for retrieval). Dir added to `.gitignore`.

### CLI
`axiom failure-classification-create [--classifications-file <f>] [--json-output]`, `failure-classification-show <report_id>`, `failure-classification-export <report_id>` — registered in `runner/command_registry.py` (READ_ONLY/SAFE) and `tests/test_command_registry.py`'s expected set. `show`/`export` exit `2` on not-found, `1` on invalid id.

## Behavior changed / not changed
- **Changed**: new module, 3 new CLI commands, 3 new registry entries, 1 new test-selection mapping.
- **Not changed**: existing PR #29 failure-classification module + tests untouched; no capability/agent/adapter/service touched; Revit 2024 baseline untouched; no new dependencies; no network calls.

## Non-Goals
No repair engine, schedulers, worker orchestration, autonomous execution, architecture changes, or broad refactoring.

## Validation
- `poetry run pytest -q` → **3588 passed, 1 skipped** (+37 new tests; baseline after #112 merge was 3551 passed, 1 skipped).
- `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` → **0 errors**.
- Manual CLI smoke (create → show → export, severity+category output, error paths, evidence files written) verified.

## Risks
Low. Self-contained, deterministic, read-only governance module following an established, tested pattern. No Revit live validation required; 2024 baseline unaffected.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
